# 6. Visualizing Data in jamovi

Visualizations help us see our data. Descriptive statistics are useful, but they can hide important patterns. A graph can reveal skew, outliers, unusual categories, group differences, or data entry problems that might not be obvious from a table of numbers.

Visualizations are not separate from descriptive statistics. They help you understand the patterns behind the numbers. A mean and standard deviation can summarize a variable, but a graph can show whether the distribution is skewed, whether there are outliers, whether groups overlap, or whether something strange is happening in the data.

In other words: descriptive statistics summarize; visualizations help you see.

This chapter focuses on choosing and interpreting common visualizations in jamovi.

By the end of this chapter, you should be able to:

- explain why visualizing data is an important part of analysis
- choose graphs based on variable type and research question
- create and interpret graphs for continuous variables
- create and interpret graphs for categorical variables
- create and interpret graphs for continuous variables across groups
- use graphs to identify patterns, outliers, and possible data problems
